description:
Flag bursty buckets in an event-rate series. target.kind:
'edge_event_rate' (optionally per rel_type) or 'node_activity'
(requires target.uid; counts incident events). method 'zscore' flags
|x - trailing_mean| >= z * trailing_std over the previous params.w
buckets (default w=10, z=3); 'ratio' flags x / trailing_median >= r.
Returns only flagged buckets, with scores. Cost: cheap.
Example: {"target": {"kind": "edge_event_rate"}, "window": {...},
          "stride": 86400000000, "method": "zscore"}
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor, n_buckets.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: burst_detection

description:
Interval join between two edge-version sets selected by a_spec/b_spec
{src?, dst?, rel_type?} under a strict Allen relation on valid time:
overlaps (a starts first, they overlap, b ends last), during (a inside
b), meets (a ends exactly when b starts), before (a ends, then b starts
within `gap`; gap required). Cost: moderate; narrow the specs.
Example: {"a_spec": {"src": "a", "rel_type": "R"},
          "b_spec": {"rel_type": "S"},
          "allen_relation": {"relation": "before", "gap": 3600000000}}
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor.

a_spec.description: edge-version selector; null fields match anything

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

b_spec.description: edge-version selector; null fields match anything

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: co_active

description:
Deterministic computation over a prior step's rows — never do arithmetic
yourself. fn: count (rows), sum/min/max (numeric `field`), topk (`field`,
`k` -> best rows), filter (`field`, `cmp` in eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge/contains,
`value`), interval_relation (`a`, `b` half-open intervals -> Allen
relation name). `input` normally arrives via {"$ref": "sN.rows"}.
Cost: trivial. Example: {"fn": "topk", "input": {"$ref": "s2.rows"},
"field": "value", "k": 3}
Output fields: value, rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor.

input.description: rows or scalars from a prior step ($ref)

name: compute

description:
Exact count of delta-temporal motif instances among edge events in
`window`: motif edges strictly time-ordered with span <= delta
(microseconds), motif nodes pairwise distinct, rel_type ignored.
Motifs: M_triangle_cyclic (u->v,v->w,w->u), M_triangle_acyclic_1
(u->v,u->w,v->w), M_2node_pingpong (u->v,v->u,u->v), M_star_out_3
(u->a,u->b,u->c), M_path_3 (u->v->w->x).
Cost: heavy — REQUIRES node_filter (<= 10k uids) on large graphs;
E_COST tells you when. Example: {"motif": "M_triangle_cyclic",
"delta": 86400000000, "window": {...}, "node_filter": ["a","b","c"]}
Output fields: count, n_events_in_window, truncated.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

node_filter.description: restrict all motif nodes to this uid set

name: count_temporal_motifs

description:
What changed between the snapshots at t1 and t2 under one belief state:
nodes/edges added & removed (by logical identity) and props_changed.
Use `scope` {seeds, hops} to restrict to a neighborhood — strongly
recommended on large graphs. Each list is capped at `limit` and reports
a *_total count. Cost: two interval-pruned scans (moderate; scope helps).
Example: {"t1": 1580515200000000, "t2": 1583020800000000,
          "scope": {"seeds": ["acme-corp"], "hops": 2}}
Output fields: nodes_added, nodes_added_total, nodes_removed, nodes_removed_total, edges_added, edges_added_total, edges_removed, edges_removed_total, props_changed, props_changed_total, truncated.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

scope.description: restrict the diff to nodes within `hops` of `seeds` in either snapshot (edges need both endpoints in scope)

name: diff_snapshots

description:
Full version history of one node: every version of `uid` believed at
`as_of_tt` (default: current beliefs), ordered by valid-time start; set
include_edges=true to also get its incident edge versions.
Constraints: uid must come from resolve_entities or the task input.
Cost: cheap (single-identity lookup; incident edges add one scan).
Example: {"uid": "acme-corp", "include_edges": true, "limit": 50}
-> rows: [{vid, label, vt_s, vt_e, tt_s, tt_e, props}, ...]
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor, edges, edges_truncated.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: entity_history

description:
Like count_temporal_motifs but returns the instances themselves as
ordered edge-event tuples, paginated and deterministically ordered.
Use to name participants after counting. Same cost caveats.
Example adds: {"limit": 20} -> rows: [{edges: [{src,dst,t,eid,rel_type}
x3]}, ...]
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

node_filter.description: restrict all motif nodes to this uid set

name: find_temporal_motif_instances

description:
Per-bucket metric series over `window` with bucket width `stride`
(bucket count <= 2000). Metrics: node_count / active_edge_count /
mean_out_degree sample the bucket start; edge_event_count /
new_node_rate / reciprocity aggregate events inside the bucket.
Cost: one interval-pruned scan — cheap.
Example: {"metric": "edge_event_count", "window": {...},
          "stride": 604800000000}  # weekly buckets
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor, n_buckets.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: graph_metric_timeseries

description:
How `uid`'s neighborhood changed between t1 and t2: neighbors gained
and lost (undirected), plus an incident-active-edge-count series
sampled every `stride` (default (t2-t1)/20). Thin composition of
diff + timeseries scoped to one node — prefer it over manual diffs.
Cost: cheap. Example: {"uid": "acme-corp", "t1": ..., "t2": ...}
Output fields: neighbors_gained, neighbors_gained_total, neighbors_lost, neighbors_lost_total, degree_series, stride, truncated.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

name: neighborhood_evolution

description:
Look up node uids by name: exact uid match first, then case-insensitive
substring on uid and on the `name` property of any believed version.
ALWAYS use this to obtain uids — never invent identifiers; plans with
literal uids not from the task input or a prior step are rejected.
Cost: cheap. Example: {"query": "ACME", "label": "Org", "limit": 5}
-> rows: [{uid, label, name, match}, ...]
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: resolve_entities

description:
The k-hop neighborhood (undirected expansion, hops <= 3) of `seeds` in
the snapshot graph at instant `t_valid`, under belief state `as_of_tt`;
returns induced edges (paginated `rows`) and nodes with hop distance.
Cost: cheap for hops <= 2; hop 3 on hubs can be large — lower `limit`.
Example: {"seeds": ["acme-corp"], "hops": 2, "t_valid": 1580515200000000}
-> rows: [{eid, src, dst, rel_type, vt_s, vt_e}, ...], nodes: [...]
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor, nodes, nodes_total, nodes_truncated.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: snapshot_subgraph

description:
Up to k (<= 20) concrete node-simple time-respecting paths src -> dst
inside `window`, hop cap 6, ordered by (arrival, hops). Use after
temporal_reachability confirms dst is reachable. Cost: exponential in
max_hops — keep max_hops <= 4 and windows narrow.
Example: {"src": "a", "dst": "d", "k": 3, "max_hops": 4,
          "window": {"t_a": 0, "t_b": 1700000000000000}}
-> rows: [{arrival, hops, edges: [{src, dst, rel_type, t, eid}, ...]}]
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

name: temporal_paths

description:
Which nodes are reachable from `src` via time-respecting paths (edge
times non-decreasing) inside `window` — returns earliest arrival per
node, source excluded, sorted by arrival. direction='in' answers "who
could reach src"; delta_max_wait caps the wait at intermediate nodes
(exact path semantics). Cost: linear in window edges without delta;
with delta it can be much heavier — keep windows narrow.
Example: {"src": "acme-corp",
          "window": {"t_a": 1580515200000000, "t_b": 1583020800000000},
          "direction": "out"}
-> rows: [{uid, earliest_arrival}, ...]
Output fields: rows, rows_total, truncated, cursor.

as_of_tt.description: belief state to evaluate under; OPEN_END = current beliefs

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a previous call

name: temporal_reachability
